# aikido_code_repo_config (Resource)

Manages the scanning configuration of an existing code repository in Aikido Security. The repository itself is imported from your Git provider — this resource controls its scanning settings.

## Example Usage

```terraform
data "aikido_code_repos" "all" {}

locals {
  api_server = one([for r in data.aikido_code_repos.all.repos : r if r.name == "yulife-api-server"])
}

resource "aikido_code_repo_config" "api_server" {
  code_repo_id             = local.api_server.id
  active                   = true
  sensitivity              = "extreme"
  connectivity             = "connected"
  dev_dep_scanning_enabled = true
  excluded_paths           = ["vendor/", "node_modules/", "test/fixtures/"]
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `code_repo_id` (String) The ID of the code repository to manage.

### Optional

- `active` (Boolean) Whether scanning is active for this repository.
- `connectivity` (String) The connectivity status: `connected`, `not_connected`, or `unknown`.
- `dev_dep_scanning_enabled` (Boolean) Whether development dependency scanning is enabled.
- `excluded_paths` (Set of String) Paths excluded from scanning.
- `sensitivity` (String) The sensitivity level: `extreme`, `sensitive`, `normal`, `not_sensitive`, or `no_data`.

### Read-Only

- `branch` (String) The branch being scanned.
- `id` (String) The code repository ID.
- `name` (String) The name of the code repository.
- `provider_name` (String) The Git provider (e.g., github, gitlab, bitbucket).
- `url` (String) The URL of the repository.

## Import

Import is supported using the following syntax:

The [`terraform import` command](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/cli/commands/import) can be used, for example:

```shell
# Import a code repository configuration by its repo ID
terraform import aikido_code_repo_config.api_server "410013"
```
